content.full_text:
August 15, 1989


Jerome J. Hargarten
Green Valley Civic Association
13622 Orchard Gate Rd.
Poway, CA 92064

                                        Re:  Your Request for Advice
                                             Our File No. A-89-444

Dear Mr. Hargarten:

     We have received your letter of July 24, 1989, seeking
written confirmation of the advice I provided to you in a
telephone conversation of July 21, 1989, regarding the Green
Valley Civic Association's filing obligations under the Political
Reform Act of 1974 (Government Code Sections 81000-91015).  This
is to confirm that your letter accurately reflects that advice.

     A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience.

     If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        By:  Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
                                             Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure




428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

GREEN VALLEY
CIVIC ASSOCIATION

FPPC

July 24, 1989

JUL 26 2:50 PM '89

CERTIFIED MAIL

Mr. Kevin Braaten-Moen
Fair Political Practices Committee
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Braaten-Moen:

This is to confirm your advice given by telephone on July 21,
1989 re the question of the Green Valley Civic Association's
(GVCA) campaign disclosure obligations under the provisions of
the California Political Reform Act.

My call to your office was prompted by recent GVCA expenditures
for organizing communications to Poway City Council re community
opposition to a landowner's proposal for a rezoning which could
fall under a city ordinance enacted in 1988. The ordinance
provides that, if City Council votes in favor of permitting
increased residential density on land zoned "rural residential",
implementation of such rezoning requires voter approval.

You advised me that the above described activity and expenditures
by GVCA do not fall under the campaign disclosures provision of
the California Political Reform Act and that, therefore, no
reporting is required.

However, as I understand your advice, if the GVCA were to collect
or expend funds for the purpose of opposing or supporting a
ballot measure or of opposing or supporting placing a measure on
a ballot (via the petition-initiative process), it would become
subject to the campaign disclosure requirements. In that event,
considering that GVCA's purpose is to bring about civic better-
ment and social improvements, for the purpose of reporting GVCA
would be considered a "General Purpose Recipient Committee".

You offered that, if and when GVCA becomes involved in such
activity, you or someone else in your office would assist us in
the selection and preparation of the required reports.

The GVCA and I are grateful for your assistance and ask that if
any of the above is inaccurate or incorrect, you advise me at
your earliest convenience.

Very truly yours,

Jerome J. Hargarten,
Vice President

13622 Orchard Gate Rd.
Poway, CA 92064

July 28, 1989


Jerome J. Hargarten
Vice President
Green Valley Civic Association
13622 Orchard Gate Road
Poway, CA  92064

                                        Re:  Letter No. 89-444

Dear Mr. Hargarten:

     We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice
under the Political Reform Act on July 21, 1989.  Your letter has
been assigned to our Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
for response.  If you have any questions, you may contact tht
division directly at (916) 322-5662.

     If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response.  A confirming
response will be released after it has gone through our approval
process.  If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you
shortly to advise you.  In such cases, the normal analysis, review
and approval process will be followed.

     You should be aware that your letter and our response are
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

KED:plh:confadvl


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: Does the Green Valley Civic Association's expenditures for organizing communications to Poway City Council regarding community opposition to a rezoning proposal fall under the campaign disclosure provisions of the California Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The GVCA's current expenditures for organizing communications to the city council regarding the rezoning proposal do not trigger campaign disclosure requirements. However, if GVCA collects or expends funds to support or oppose a ballot measure or ballot measure placement, it would become subject to campaign disclosure requirements and be classified as a "General Purpose Recipient Committee."
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document consists of three parts: (1) FPPC's August 15, 1989 confirmation letter; (2) July 24, 1989 requestor letter confirming telephone advice; (3) July 28, 1989 FPPC acknowledgment letter. The actual substantive advice is contained in the requestor's letter dated July 24, which confirms the telephone conversation from July 21. OCR quality is fair with some character recognition issues.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: This is to confirm your advice given by telephone on July 21, 1989 re the question of the Green Valley Civic Association's (GVCA) campaign disclosure obligations under the provisions of the California Political Reform Act. My call to your office was prompted by recent GVCA expenditures for organizing communications to Poway City Council re community opposition to a landowner's proposal for a rezoning which could fall under a city ordinance enacted in 1988. The ordinance provides that, if City Council votes in favor of permitting increased residential density on land zoned "rural residential", implementation of such rezoning requires voter approval.

CONCLUSION: You advised me that the above described activity and expenditures by GVCA do not fall under the campaign disclosures provision of the California political Reform Act and that, therefore, no reporting is required. However, as I understand your advice, if the GVCA were to collect or expend funds for the purpose of opposing or supporting a ballot measure or of opposing or supporting placing a measure on a ballot (via the petition-initiative process), it would become subject to the campaign disclosure requirements.

Your letter
embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms advice provided to the Green Valley Civic Association regarding whether their expenditures for organizing communications opposing a rezoning proposal constitute reportable campaign activity under the Political Reform Act. The advice indicates that the current civic association activity does not require campaign disclosure reporting, but future ballot measure activity would trigger reporting obligations.
